body.title: CLOSED: NE task boundary flush on focus displacement
body.result: task_boundary_at on displacement; NE flushes last_narrative on task start; 10 tests pass
body.description:
When focus_state displacement occurs (new focus exceeds 1.2× hysteresis threshold), record task_boundary_at timestamptz on the focus_state row. NE checks at context assembly time: if task_boundary_at > last_ne_run_time, treat as task start (flush episodic, fresh TWM scan, no prior-turn context carried). If not, treat as step continuation (same focus, same context accumulation). Implements task vs. step distinction from AgentSPEX: task = fresh window on focus transition; step = accumulates within same focus.

**Affected files:** `wild_igor/igor/cognition/focus_state.py` (task_boundary_at column + displacement update), `wild_igor/igor/cognition/narrative_engine.py` (task boundary check in context assembly), DB migration (task_boundary_at on instance.focus_state)
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; direct psycopg2; no cortex.py touch; task_boundary_at is a timestamp (not a flag — avoids boolean state bugs); NE context flush is additive change only
**Scope boundary:** IN — task_boundary_at, displacement signal in focus_state, NE context flush check. OUT — no changes to TWM scan queries, no changes to memory retrieval.
**Test plan:** displacement triggers task_boundary_at update; NE sees task_boundary_at > last_run → fresh context assembly; no displacement → task_boundary_at not updated → step continuation
